title: Network Operating Systems: 1998 Practice Summary
author: Aberdeen Group
abstract: Aberdeen Group's 1998 practice summary on Network Operating Systems (NOS) covering Microsoft Windows NT Server
importance: and IBM OS/2 Warp Server. The report addresses key strategic questions about NOS viability in an Internet era and assesses the competitive landscape among the three major NOS vendors. Aberdeen finds that NOSs remain essential even as Internet infrastructure grows and that Novell's market leadership will be tested by the NetWare 5 launch.
relevance_rationale: Documents the pivotal 1998 NOS competitive landscape at the height of the Windows NT versus NetWare war; useful historical record of the period.
prescience_rationale: NOS market was subsequently consolidated under Microsoft; NetWare and OS/2 Warp are effectively defunct making much content of only historical interest.
column18: Correctly anticipated NT Server enterprise limitations and Novell's ongoing relevance; accurately predicted NT 5.0 as a 1999-not-1998 release; NetWare 5 launch success prediction proved accurate.
